items.title: Run oxlint before ESLint to cut monorepo lint time
items.why: Lint is often the slowest low-signal CI step; a Rust linter as first pass catches most issues in seconds so ESLint only runs on the remainder.
how_to_steps: Install oxlint as a dev dependency: `pnpm add -D oxlint` (or bun/npm equivalent).
Add `"lint:fast": "oxlint"` and change CI to run `oxlint` before `eslint`.
Migrate overlapping rules with `@oxlint/migrate` or use `eslint-plugin-oxlint` to disable duplicates in ESLint.
Wire oxlint into Turbo as a cached task so unchanged packages skip re-lint on cache hit.
items.evidence: Oxlint benchmarks show 50–100× faster than ESLint on equivalent rule sets; a monorepo team reported oxlint as a 30–40% per-job speedup as a drop-in pre-check.
items.try_today: Add oxlint to one repo's CI and compare lint-stage duration on the next green PR.

items.title: Give each parallel agent its own git worktree under Turbo remote cache
items.why: Multiple agents on one checkout thrash the working tree and bust caches; worktrees isolate branches while Turbo 3 shares remote cache across them.
how_to_steps: Enable Turborepo remote caching (`turbo login`, `turbo link`, set `TURBO_TOKEN`/`TURBO_TEAM` in CI).
For each agent task, `git worktree add ../agent-N feat/agent-N` instead of switching branches in-place.
Run `turbo run lint typecheck test build --filter=...[origin/main]` inside each worktree.
Remove worktrees when done: `git worktree remove ../agent-N`.
items.evidence: Turborepo 3.0 documents worktree-safe shared cache; a 4-app monorepo case study reports cold CI ~120s vs warm ~12s with parallel agent worktrees.
items.try_today: Spin up two worktrees on one repo and confirm both hit the same remote Turbo cache on an unchanged package.
